pi_items.source: Task description (line 5) states 'Record the EXACT error message text displayed for each case into a file at /sdcard/error_comparison.txt'. Scripts confirm this path is used in export_result.sh and setup_task.sh

Task description (line 5) explicitly states: 'Enter this-is-not-an-email to trigger a format error'. Verified as malformed because: (1) lacks @ symbol required in RFC 5322 email format, (2) no domain portion, making it invalid per email specification standards

Task description (line 5) explicitly states: 'Enter ghost_user_999@example.com to trigger a user not found error'. Verified as valid email format (has @, domain, TLD) and appears to be deliberately crafted test email to not exist in the Flight Crew View user database (prefix 'ghost_user_999' is a clear test pattern)

privileged_info_summary: The task tests error handling in Flight Crew View app (com.robert.fcView), a real published Android application for airline crew scheduling. Test inputs are synthetic: 'this-is-not-an-email' is malformed (no @ symbol, violates RFC 5322), and 'ghost_user_999@example.com' is a valid email format designed to test non-existent user error. Output path '/sdcard/error_comparison.txt' is a standard Android external storage location. All metadata claims are verified and accurately describe test configuration parameters.
